festivals.name: Las Fallas
festivals.description: A explosive week of fireworks, parades, and giant satirical papier-mâché sculptures that are ultimately set ablaze in a fiery climax.

festivals.name: Semana Santa
festivals.description: Holy Week processions featuring elaborate floats of religious icons carried through streets by penitents in traditional robes.

festivals.name: Feria de Abril
festivals.description: A vibrant week-long fair with flamenco dancing, colorful traditional dress, horse parades, and late-night celebrations in decorated tents.

festivals.name: San Isidro Festival
festivals.description: Madrid's patron saint festival combining traditional verbena celebrations, concerts, and some of the year's most prestigious bullfights.

festivals.name: Feria de Córdoba
festivals.description: A lively spring fair showcasing Andalusian culture with flamenco, horse shows, and the city's famous flower-filled patios open to visitors.

festivals.name: San Juan
festivals.description: Midsummer night celebrations with beach bonfires, fireworks, and traditional rituals to welcome the summer solstice.

festivals.name: San Fermín
festivals.description: World-famous festival featuring the daring Running of the Bulls through narrow streets alongside nightly festivities and traditional Basque culture.

festivals.name: La Tomatina
festivals.description: A chaotic and joyful food fight festival where thousands of participants throw ripe tomatoes at each other in the streets.

festivals.name: Feria de Málaga
festivals.description: A massive celebration blending daytime historic-center festivities with nighttime fairground fun, flamenco, and Andalusian traditions.

festivals.name: La Mercè
festivals.description: Barcelona's largest festival featuring human towers (castells), fire runs, concerts, and spectacular fireworks celebrating the city's patron saint.

festivals.name: Fiesta Nacional de España
festivals.description: Spain's national day marked by a grand military parade in Madrid and patriotic celebrations across the country.

festivals.name: All Saints' Day
festivals.description: A solemn national holiday when families visit cemeteries to honor deceased loved ones and enjoy traditional seasonal pastries.

festivals.name: Feria de Todos los Santos
festivals.description: A quieter off-season cultural festival celebrating the island's traditions, crafts, and culinary heritage away from the summer crowds.

festivals.name: Christmas Markets
festivals.description: Festive markets filled with handcrafted ornaments, nativity figures, mulled wine, and seasonal treats throughout the holiday season.

festivals.name: Nochevieja
festivals.place: Madrid's Puerta del Sol and nationwide
festivals.description: New Year's Eve celebrations centered on the tradition of eating twelve grapes at the stroke of midnight for good luck.

festivals.name: Three Kings Day
festivals.description: A beloved holiday marked by elaborate evening parades the night before, as the Three Wise Men bring gifts to children across Spain.
